text: You fly like both your hands are arguing about who gets the damn controls.
Your map awareness is so bad hostiles send you a damn fruit basket for the free kills.
You're the only bastard alive who can lose a fight to an asteroid.
Your aim is so shit the target has to fly into your bullets out of pity.
You make decisions like a damn magic eight ball that only says worse idea.
Hell, even the loading screen plays better than you do.
You warp to zero on everything like keeping range is some elitist bullshit.
Your kill-death ratio looks like a fraction that failed math class, dumbass.
You couldn't find the enemy fleet with a map, a scout, and a big damn arrow.
You tackle friendlies so often the corp thinks you switched sides, dumbass.
Your overview settings are a war crime and your piloting is the damn trial.
You've got the reflexes of a parked shuttle and the awareness of a damn rock.
You play like the keyboard owes you money and you're beating it out one wrong damn key at a time.
Your fitting choices make the ship weep actual damn coolant.
You'd lose a race to a stationary object and blame the fucking lag.
Hell, you couldn't hold a gate if it came with handles.
Your battle plan has two steps: die, then ask what the hell happened in fleet chat.
You alt-tab mid-fight so damn much the enemy scored you as two separate bad players.
The killboard flagged your account as a damn charity.
You aim like the crosshair is decorative, you beautiful disaster of a bastard.
Your escape plan is always panic in a straight line, and it has never fucking worked.
You've been kited by players, NPCs, and one particularly motivated cargo container, dumbass.
Your threat assessment is a coin flip where both sides say attack, and both are wrong as hell.
You bet the whole fleet on a hunch, and your hunches are fucking terrible.
You navigate by vibes, and your vibes have a shit sense of direction.
Your ego has its own gravity well and your gameplay is the damn space junk orbiting it.
Your idea of intel is screaming guys, thirty seconds after you're already dead, dumbass.
You could trip over a flat floor in zero gravity, you absolute damn marvel.
Your DPS is theoretical, just like your damn game sense.
The minimap is right there and you treat it like spoilers, dumbass.
You lock the wrong target so often the right one gets jealous as hell.
You panic so fast the fight hasn't even started and you're already damn gone.
Your piloting has the grace of a shopping cart with one bastard wheel.
You die to fire on the ground in every game that has fire and ground, dumbass.
Your clutch plays are so rare they belong in a damn museum.
You call primaries like a broken clock, except the clock is right twice a damn day.
Hell, you'd get lost in a one-system region with the gate lit up like a casino.
Your reflexes ship by freighter, with three damn stops on the way.
You loot the field before the fight ends, and the fight always fucking objects.
You fly expensive ships like you hate money and the money hates you back, damn.
Your positioning is so bad the enemy FC uses you as a damn waypoint.
You facecheck every corner like the game owes you a jump scare, dumbass.
Even your damn escape pod files a complaint every time you undock.
You're the reason the fleet channel needs a no, the other left macro, dumbass.
Your game sense clocked out mid-fight and left a note that just says good fucking luck.
You've got two speeds: too late, and what the fuck was that.
You follow orders like a shopping list you left at home, dumbass.
Your bravado-to-skill ratio would break a damn calculator.
You read the battle like a menu in a language you failed, dumbass.
Hell, you could tank a compliment and still lose structure.
You yell for backup before the enemy even loads in, you jumpy bastard.
Your best highlight this month was the damn server maintenance screen.
You treat cooldowns as suggestions and dying as a lifestyle, dumbass.
Your aim has the consistency of wet cardboard in a damn hurricane.
You chase kills into the sun like a moth with a damn mortgage.
The enemy scout doesn't report your movements; the poor bastard just laughs into the mic.
You'd need a tutorial for the damn tutorial.
Your fleet position is technically everywhere except where you were told, dumbass.
You panic-jump into the one system everybody said to avoid, every damn time.
Your trigger discipline is a rumor and your restraint is a damn myth.
You get outplayed by the environment more than by the actual damn players.
Hell, gravity has beaten you more times than any enemy pilot ever has.
You call it a flank; everyone else calls it wandering off to die alone, dumbass.
Your keybinds are a crime scene and your muscle memory is the prime damn suspect.
You spectate your own gameplay in real time, you glorious bastard.
Your warp-ins are so damn bad they should come with an apology letter.
You mistake confidence for cover, and cover for optional, dumbass.
Even the respawn timer is tired of your shit.
You've made feeding an art form; shame it hangs in the enemy's damn gallery.
Your comms are ninety percent wait, what, and ten percent explosions, dumbass.
You couldn't secure an objective with the whole enemy team on a damn lunch break.
Your pathing looks like you're avoiding an invisible bee, you weird bastard.
You hold the throttle like a support beam and your brain like it's optional, dumbass.
Hell, you've been countered by an automatic door.
Your big-brain plays need a much bigger damn brain.
You strafe directly into the shots like they're handing out prizes, dumbass.
Your risk assessment is just vibes and hull damage, damn.
Your losing streak has a fan club, and you're the damn president.
You farm deaths like it's a side hustle with great fucking benefits.
Your target calling is inspirational, in that it mostly inspires the damn enemy.
You lose duels to people who are alt-tabbed, you poor bastard.
Your escape routes all lead straight through the enemy fleet, every damn time.
Hell, you couldn't ambush a sleeping NPC with the sound off.
You take fights like you take advice: badly, and at full fucking speed.
Your cargo hold has more sense than your damn flight plan.
You're a masterclass in what the hell not to do, taught live, nightly.
Your duel record is so bad the arena offered you a damn participation refund.
You dodge like the incoming fire has your home address, dumbass.
You'd struggle in creative mode, and that's saying something, damn.
Your battle stations look a lot like sitting still and hoping, you optimistic bastard.
You shoot the wrong target with the confidence of a bastard who read the wrong briefing.
Your genius plays and your disasters are the same damn clips.
Your instincts scream retreat, you overrule them, and you lose to everyone, dumbass.
You uninstalled skill and kept the damn ego.
You've got a PhD in dying to shit everyone else saw coming.
Your gameplay is a public service announcement with explosions, you absolute damn legend of failure.
